concepts.name: Probability
concepts.short_definition: The measure of the likelihood that an event will occur.

concepts.name: Event
concepts.short_definition: A specific result or occurrence in a probability experiment.

concepts.name: Sample Space
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible outcomes of a random experiment.

concepts.name: Conditional Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of an event occurring given that another event has already occurred.

concepts.name: Joint Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of two events occurring simultaneously.

concepts.name: Marginal Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of a single event occurring, irrespective of other events.

concepts.name: Independence
concepts.short_definition: When the occurrence of one event does not affect the probability of another.

concepts.name: Dependence
concepts.short_definition: When the occurrence of one event influences the probability of another.

concepts.name: Law of Total Probability
concepts.short_definition: The sum of probabilities of all mutually exclusive and exhaustive events equals 1.

concepts.name: Bayes' Theorem
concepts.short_definition: A formula that describes the probability of an event based on prior knowledge of conditions that might be related to the event.

concepts.name: Prior Probability
concepts.short_definition: The initial probability of an event before considering new evidence.

concepts.name: Posterior Probability
concepts.short_definition: The updated probability of an event after considering new evidence.

concepts.name: Likelihood
concepts.short_definition: The probability of observing the data given a specific hypothesis or parameter value.

concepts.name: Evidence
concepts.short_definition: The probability of the observed data, averaged over all possible hypotheses.

concepts.name: Hypothesis
concepts.short_definition: A proposed explanation or statement that can be tested.

concepts.name: Data
concepts.short_definition: Information collected from an experiment or observation.

concepts.name: Updating Probabilities
concepts.short_definition: The process of revising probability estimates as new evidence becomes available.

concepts.name: Diagnostic Testing
concepts.short_definition: Using tests to determine the presence or absence of a condition.

concepts.name: Sensitivity
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a test correctly identifies a positive case.

concepts.name: Specificity
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a test correctly identifies a negative case.

concepts.name: False Positive Rate
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a test incorrectly identifies a negative case as positive.

concepts.name: False Negative Rate
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a test incorrectly identifies a positive case as negative.

concepts.name: Prevalence
concepts.short_definition: The proportion of a population that has a specific condition at a given time.

concepts.name: Positive Predictive Value
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a positive test result truly indicates the presence of the condition.

concepts.name: Negative Predictive Value
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a negative test result truly indicates the absence of the condition.

concepts.name: Bayesian Inference
concepts.short_definition: A statistical method that uses Bayes' Theorem to update probabilities based on new data.

concepts.name: Statistical Model
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical representation of a real-world process or phenomenon.

concepts.name: Parameter Estimation
concepts.short_definition: The process of estimating the values of parameters in a statistical model.

concepts.name: Machine Learning
concepts.short_definition: A field of artificial intelligence that enables systems to learn from data.

concepts.name: Classification
concepts.short_definition: Assigning data points to predefined categories or classes.

concepts.name: Naïve Bayes Classifier
concepts.short_definition: A simple probabilistic classifier based on applying Bayes' Theorem with strong independence assumptions.

concepts.name: Spam Filtering
concepts.short_definition: Using algorithms to identify and separate unwanted email messages.

concepts.name: Text Classification
concepts.short_definition: Categorizing text documents into predefined groups.

concepts.name: Causal Inference
concepts.short_definition: Determining cause-and-effect relationships between variables.

concepts.name: A/B Testing
concepts.short_definition: Comparing two versions of something to see which performs better.

concepts.name: Decision Theory
concepts.short_definition: A framework for making optimal decisions under uncertainty.

concepts.name: Risk Assessment
concepts.short_definition: Evaluating the likelihood and impact of potential negative events.

concepts.name: Bayesian Networks
concepts.short_definition: Probabilistic graphical models that represent conditional dependencies between variables.

concepts.name: Probabilistic Reasoning
concepts.short_definition: Reasoning with uncertain information using probability theory.
